name: Netherlands

colors.symbolism: replaced the original orange of the Prinsenvlag during the 17th century
from the livery of William of Orange
from the livery of William of Orange

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — red over white over blue.
history: The Dutch tricolour is one of the oldest tricolours in continuous use. Its predecessor, the orange-white-blue Prinsenvlag, was used by the forces of William the Silent during the Dutch Revolt from about 1572. By the mid-17th century the orange stripe had largely been replaced by red in usage; the States-General formally fixed the colours as red-white-blue on 19 February 1937 by royal decree of Queen Wilhelmina, ending centuries of informal practice. The exact colour shades — bright vermilion and cobalt blue — were standardised by royal decree of Queen Juliana on 17 February 1958.

features.symbolism: evolved from the orange-white-blue Prinsenvlag of William of Orange, in use during the Dutch Revolt against Spain; the orange stripe gradually shifted to red over the 17th century, possibly because the dye was more stable at sea
